上周发布的学术研究看了通过现代iOS和Android设备发送的遥测流量回到Apple和Google服务器，发现Google收集来自Android设备的遥测数据约20倍，而不是iOS。
 这项研究由都柏林大学Trinity College的Douglas J. Leith教授进行的，分析了来自手机操作的各个阶段的IOS和Android设备的流量，例如数据的各个阶段，例如共享数据：
  Leith教授表示，该研究考虑到操作系统本身可以由OS制造商-SUCH提供的默认应用程序收集，作为搜索（Siri，OkGoogle），云存储（iCloud，Google Drive），地图/位置服务（Apple地图，Google地图），照片存储/分析（Applephoto，Google照片） - 并将两个来源分开，仅关注在其默认状态下的操作系统的遥测中。
 这项研究出土了一些不舒服的结果。对于初学者来说，Leith教授表示，“尽管用户明确地解雇了”IOS和Google Android传输遥测，但是“选择”。“
 此外，即使用户未登录（确实即使它们从未登录过），也会发送此数据，“研究人员表示。
 下表总结了测试手机发送到Apple和Google服务器的主要数据点。
  但是，虽然爱尔兰研究人员发现Apple倾向于从IOS设备收集更多信息数据类型，但它是谷歌收集“一个特别大量的手机数据”。 
“在启动的前10分钟期间，像素手机发送约1MB的数据被发送到谷歌，与iPhone向苹果发送到苹果，”Leith教授说。
 “当手机坐着闲置时，与苹果计算机中的iPhone相比，像素每12小时向谷歌发送大约1MB的数据到谷歌，谷歌收集比Apple更多的手机数据大约20倍。”
  此数据收集过程每264秒在空闲Apple设备上进行每264秒，并在Android智能手机上进行一次255秒 - 即使在不使用手机时也大约每四个半分钟等同。
 但除了闲置状态之外，爱尔兰研究员还表示，当用户浏览其设置屏幕时，两个操作系统也将与中央服务器共享数据。
 此外，当新的SIM卡插入IOS和Android设备时，SIM详细信息几乎立即与Apple和Google共享。
 但Leith教授表示，他还观察了许多预先安装的应用程序和服务，即使在应用程序打开或使用之前，也可以与Apple和Google服务器的连接进行连接。
 “特别是，在iOS上包括Siri，Safari和iCloud，在GeeThe App，Chrome，Google Messaging，时钟和Google搜索栏中包括YouTube App，Chrome，Google文档，安全集线器，谷歌消息，时钟和谷歌搜索栏。 
都柏林大学教授说，这种扩展的数据收集至少提出了两个主要问题。首先，遥测可以用于将物理设备链接到个人详细信息，两家公司最有可能利用广告目的的数据。
 其次，遥测收集过程允许操作系统制造商根据连接和上传设备遥测到其服务器的IP地址跟踪用户的位置。
 研究人员表示，目前，如果有的话，如果有的话，用户可以防止遥测从他们的设备中收集。
  爱尔兰教授说他与他的研究结果联系过两家公司。苹果没有回应，而谷歌发出了澄清，他纳入论文。谷歌还告诉Leith教授，他们打算在他们收集的遥测数据上发布公共文档，但没有提供日期或截止日期。
 但在周一的电子邮件回复中，谷歌发言人播放了论文的调查结果，并声称研究人员发现了合法的遥测数据，有助于保持设备顺利运行。
 这项研究概述了智能手机的工作原理。现代汽车定期向汽车制造商提供有关车辆组件，安全状态和服务日程的基本数据，手机以非常相似的方式工作。本报告详细说明了这些通信，帮助确保IOS或Android软件是最新的，服务如预期工作，并且手机正在安全并有效运行。
 谷歌发言人 
此外，根据谷歌来源熟悉研究论文的评论，Android制造商还争议了本文的方法，他们通过排除某些类型的流量来索取IOS的遥测量，谷歌认为导致发现的偏斜结果Android设备比iOS收集更多数据的20倍。
 在这篇文章的出版物之后，Apple回应了其竞争对手的回应。
 “该报告将多个项目与不同的服务混淆，误解了个人位置数据的保护方式，”Apple发言人告诉唱片。 “Apple未收集与未经用户的知识或同意的个人相关联的数据。”
  其他详细信息可在标题为“移动手机隐私：测量数据IOSAND Android发送至Apple和Google”的研究文件中提供，作为可下载的PDF文档。
 去年，在2020年3月，相同教授还发表了一项研究，分析了网络浏览器收集的遥测。研究[PDF]发现勇敢收集了最小的数据，而微软的边缘和Yandex浏览器位于光谱的另一侧。
note_en:
Academic research published last week looked at the telemetry traffic sent by modern iOS and Android devices back to Apple and Google servers and found that Google collects around 20 times more telemetry data from Android devices than Apple from iOS.
 The research, conducted by Professor Douglas J. Leith from Trinity College at the University of Dublin, analyzed traffic originating from iOS and Android devices heading to Apple and Google servers at various stages of a phone’s operation, such as data shared:
  Prof. Leith said the research took into account that data could be collected by the operating system itself and by default apps provided by the OS makers —such as search (Siri, OkGoogle), cloud storage (iCloud, Google Drive), maps/location services (Apple Maps, Google Maps), photo storage/analytics (ApplePhoto, Google Photos)— and kept the two sources separate, focusing only on the operating systems’ telemetry, left in their default states.
 The study unearthed some uncomfortable results. For starters, Prof. Leith said that “ both iOS and Google Android transmit telemetry, despite the user explicitly opting out of this [option].”
 Furthermore, “ this data is sent even when a user is not logged in (indeed even if they have never logged in),” the researcher said.
 The table below summarizes the main data points that test handsets sent to Apple and Google servers.
  But while the Irish researcher found that Apple tends to collect more information data types from an iOS device, it was Google that collected “a notably larger volume of handset data.”
 “During the first 10 minutes of startup the Pixel handset sends around 1MB of data is sent to Google compared with the iPhone sending around 42KB of data to Apple,” Prof. Leith said.
 “When the handsets are sitting idle the Pixel sends roughly 1MB of data to Google every 12 hours compared with the iPhone sending 52KB to Apple i.e.,  Google collects around 20 times more handset data than Apple.”
  This data collection process takes place every 264 seconds on idle Apple devices and once 255 seconds on Android smartphones — which roughly equates to almost every four and a half minutes even when the handset is not being used.
 But in addition to the idle state, the Irish researcher said that both operating systems also share data with their central servers when users are browsing their settings screens.
 Furthermore, when a new SIM card is inserted into both iOS and Android devices, SIM details are shared with both Apple and Google almost immediately.
 But Prof. Leith said that he also observed a number of pre-installed apps and services also making connections to both Apple and Google servers even before the apps were opened or used.
 “In particular, on iOS these include Siri, Safari and iCloud and on Google Android these include the Youtube app, Chrome, Google Docs, Safety hub, Google Messaging, the Clock, and the Google Search bar,” Prof. Leith said.
 The University of Dublin professor says that this expansive data collection raises at least two major concerns. First, that the telemetry can be used to link physical devices to personal details, data that both companies are most likely exploiting for advertising purposes.
 Second, that the telemetry collection process allows the OS makers to track users’ location based on the IP address that connects and uploads device telemetry to their servers.
 The researcher said that currently, there are very few, if any, realistic options for users to prevent telemetry collection from their devices.
  The Irish professor said he contacted both companies with his findings. Apple did not respond, while Google sent clarifications, which he incorporated into the paper. Google also told Prof. Leith that they intended to publish public documentation on the telemetry data they collect, but did not provide a date or deadline.
 But in an email response on Monday, a Google spokesperson played down the paper’s findings, and claimed the researcher found legitimate telemetry data that helps keep devices running smoothly.
 This research outlines how smartphones work. Modern cars regularly send basic data about vehicle components, their safety status and service schedules to car manufacturers, and mobile phones work in very similar ways. This report details those communications, which help ensure that iOS or Android software is up to date, services are working as intended, and that the phone is secure and running efficiently.
 Google spokesperson
 In addition, according to a Google source familiar with the research paper’s review, the Android maker also disputed the paper’s methodology, which they claim under-counted iOS’ telemetry volume by excluding certain types of traffic, which Google believes resulted in skewed results that found Android devices collecting 20 times more data than iOS.
 In a response after this article’s publication, Apple echoed its rival’s response.
 “The report conflates a number of items in relation to different services and misunderstands how personal location data is protected,” an Apple spokesperson told  The Record. “Apple is not collecting data that can be associated with individuals without a user’s knowledge or consent.”
  Additional details are available in a research paper titled “ Mobile Handset Privacy: Measuring The Data iOSand Android Send to Apple And Google,” available as a  downloadable PDF document.
 Last year, in March 2020, the same professor also published a study analyzing the telemetry collected by web browsers. The study [ PDF] found that Brave collected the smallest amount of data, while Microsoft’s Edge and the Yandex Browser were at the opposite side of the spectrum.
title: Google从Android设备收集20倍的遥测，而不是iOS
title_en: Google Collects 20 Times More Telemetry From Android Devices Than Apple From iOS

note: Volkswagen AG的美国子公司周二表示，该公司将作为美国的伏尔斯华人重塑为促进其电动汽车战略，而是德国母公司的发言人后来表示这一举动是个笑话。  立即亮起社交媒体和在线新闻网站的名称变更原本是愚人节的愚人节特技，让人们谈论VW的雄心勃勃的电动汽车战略，因为公司推出了其第一款全电动运动型多用途车辆 ，ID。 4，发言人在美国经销商处表示。  大众的问题是每个人都认真对待它，为公司的意图创造了混乱，并使VW的通信团队对防御性的通信团队。  “我们不是故意误导任何人，”Wolfsburg的一位大众发言人告诉华尔街日报。 “整件事只是让人们谈论ID.4的营销行动。”
note_en:
Volkswagen AG’s U.S. subsidiary said Tuesday the company would rebrand itself as Voltswagen of America to promote its electric car strategy, but a spokesman for the parent company in Germany later said the move was a joke.
  The name change, which immediately lit up social media and online news sites, was originally intended as an early April Fools’ Day stunt to get people talking about VW’s ambitious electric car strategy as the company rolls out its first all-electric sport-utility vehicle, the ID. 4, in U.S. dealerships, the spokesman said.
  The problem for VW is that everyone took it seriously, creating confusion about the company’s intentions and moving the shares, putting VW’s communications team on the defensive.
  “We didn’t mean to mislead anyone,” a Volkswagen spokesman in Wolfsburg told The Wall Street Journal. “The whole thing is just a marketing action to get people talking about the ID.4.”
title: 大众汽车并不重新加入自动大众汽车

note:
华盛顿 - 总统拜登周三推出了2万亿美元的计划，改革并升级国家的基础设施，称之为变革，可以创造“世界上最具弹性，创新的经济”。
 “这不是一个计划在边缘周围修补，”拜登先生在匹兹堡以外的演讲中说。 “这是美国一生的一生投资。”
 白宫官员表示，该提案的支出和税收抵免的结合将转化为20,000英里的重建道路，修理全国10个最经济的重要桥梁，消除来自国家的供水和其他项目的长期列表旨在在短期内创造数百万个工作岗位，从长远来看，加强美国竞争力。
 他们表示，该计划还将通过加速转向新的，更清洁的能源来加速与气候变化的斗争，并有助于促进经济的种族平等。
 拜登先生表示，规定将提高工资，互联网服务，饮用水和通勤时期。
 15年超过15年的企业税收收入增加，特别是来自赚取的跨国公司和海外预订的跨国公司，费用将被抵消。总统将这些增加作为一种手段，以刺激在美国投资和生产更多的手段。
 与共和党人已经发挥了怀疑论者或彻底反对，拜登先生向国会双方的支持诉求，称该计划与我们在几十年前建造的公路体系和太空赛开始以来，我们所看到或所做的任何事情不同“和呼叫它是“自第二次世界大战以来的最大的美国工作投资”。 
总统说，该计划的支出将持续八年，税收增加超过15年支出的抵消，导致预算赤字的最终降低。与2009年总统在Barack Obama主席下通过的经济刺激不同，拜登先生副总统，官员不得在各种情况下优先考虑所谓的铲子准备项目，这些项目可能会迅速加强增长。
 但甚至蔓延多年来，提案的规模强调了拜登先生先生有机会使用联邦支出以在半个世纪未见的方式中解决长期社会和经济挑战的机会。
 官员表示，如果批准，该计划的支出将结束数十年的联邦研究和基础设施投资的停滞 - 并将在20世纪60年代以来其份额返回政府对这些领域的投资，以自20世纪60年代以来其最高水平。
  该提案是上半年，总统雄心勃勃的议程的两步释放，改革经济，再次召回美国资本主义，这可能在十年上携带总费用的4万亿美元。拜登先生的政府已将其命名为“美国就业计划”，竞争了1.9万亿大流行救济法案，即拜登先生本月签署了“美国救援计划”。
 星期三，拜登先生表示，下一阶段，他将致力于富含富有人的税收的税收，这将在几周内发出，被称为“美国家庭计划”。
 在道路上，桥梁和其他对国家的经济基金会的桥梁和其他物理改善始终有两头竞争的呼吁，该计划在周三推出的计划先生提出了迅速反对其规模的权利，并依赖于企业税收增加。
 共和党和商业团体批评这些税收提案，称他们为Bipartisan谈判的人。拜登先生承认批评，即使他为公司要求提供更多税收。他说，他将继续努力为他的提案赢得共和党支持。 
他说，他已经与共和党领导人的参议员Mitch McConnell曾与共和党领导人，关于该法案，并计划邀请其他共和党人邀请其他共和党人担任白宫，因为国会转向将他的提案转化为详细的立法。
 拜登先生挑战批评者，提供自己的提案来支付该计划。 “我对其他想法开放了，”他说，“只要他们不征收不到400,000美元的人征收任何税收。”
 McConnell先生在周三建议，他不太可能以白宫提出的形式支持包裹。
 “如果它将增加大规模的税收，而且增加了国民债务的数量，而且很可能是，”他说。
  该计划的支出涵盖了各种物理基础设施项目，包括运输，宽带，电网和外壳;努力跳跃启动先进制造业;其他行业官员认为，作为美国与中国经济竞争日益增长的关键。
 它还包括列车数百万工人的资金，以及支持劳动工会和家庭私人提供者提供的倡议的资金，同时还增加了提供该护理的工人的工资。
 计划中的许多项目载有价格标签，这些标签将在过去的主管部门中填补整个雄心勃勃的票据。 
其中：共有1800亿美元的研发，道路和桥梁1150亿美元，公共交通的850亿美元，amtrak和货运铁路的800亿美元。港口和机场有420亿美元，宽带1000亿美元，用于水资源基础设施的1110亿美元 - 包括450亿美元，以确保没有儿童被迫从铅管中喝水，这可以减缓儿童的发展并导致行为和其他问题。 。
  该计划旨在修复全国各地10,000名较小的桥梁，以及需要修复的10个最经济的重要人物。它将为国家的黄色校车队的20％带电。它将花费3000亿美元来促进先进的制造，其中包括为该国的战略国民储存的药物，包括疫苗，为未来流行病提供了四年的计划。
 在许多情况下，官员在经济中闭上种族差距的语言演绎了这些目标，有时是之前的联邦支出的结果，如州际公路发展，这些公路发展拆分了影响港口或权力附近的黑人和西班牙裔社区的颜色或空气污染的社区植物。
 官员们将4000亿美元的支出，部分照顾是那些产业中的“欠款和低估”的工人，他们是不成比例的妇女的颜色。
  拜登先生在整个计划中嵌入了解决气候变化的承诺。道路，桥梁和机场将为温暖的星球锻造更多极端风暴，洪水和火灾的影响。研究和开发的支出可以帮助尖端清洁技术进行突破，而计划改造和融化数百万建筑物会使它们更节能。
 然而，总统对气候变化的关注是以现代化和转变的最大来源的全球温控温室气体污染：汽车和电力厂。
 它担心担心支出意味着增加电动汽车的使用，今天占美国高速公路的2％的车辆。 
该计划提出了17.4亿美元，鼓励通过授予美国在美国而不是中国电动汽车电池的公司制造和购买电动汽车的制造和购买电动汽车。目标是减少车辆价格标签。
  这笔钱也将资助建设大约一百万电动车辆充电站 - 尽管专家表示，在制造电动汽车的主流选择所需的一小部分中，这是一个主流选择所需的一小部分。
 拜登先生的计划提出了1000亿美元的计划，以更新和现代化电网，使其更加可靠，更不易被停电，就像最近德克萨斯州的那些，同时也在大城市到大城市建造更多的传输线。
 它建议创造“清洁电力标准” - 基本上，联邦授权要求美国在风，太阳能和可能核电等零碳能源产生的一定比例的电力。但该授权必须由国会颁布，在那里其成功的前景保持朦胧。在过去的20年里，通过这种任务的类似努力失败了。
 该计划提出了额外的460亿美元的联邦采购方案，为政府机构购买电动汽车车队，以及用于尖端新技术的研发计划350亿美元。
 它还要求制定基础设施和社区，为气候变化的恶化效果更加努力，虽然政府迄今为止提供了一些关于如何实现这一目标的细节。
 但根据白宫发布的文件，该计划包括500亿美元“在专业投资中，以改善基础设施恢复力。”努力将防御野火，崛起的海洋和飓风，并将重点关注保护低收入居民和颜色人民的投资。 
该计划还包括160亿美元的计划，旨在帮助化石燃料工作人员转向新的工作 - 就像盖帽上的新工作泄漏，并关闭退休的煤矿 - 为新的“民用气候军团100亿美元”。
  拜登先生将通过消除对化石燃料生产商的税收优惠来组成他的支出。但他的税收增加通常会来自公司。
 他将使公司税率从21％提高到28％，部分扭转了唐纳德J.特朗普总统签署了法律的削减。拜登先生还将采取各种步骤筹集跨国公司的税收，其中许多工作在大修范围内，于2017年纳税人税收先生所纳入的海外税收。
 这些措施包括提高全球利润最低税率，并消除若干条款，使公司能够减少他们获得的利润和国外预订的税收责任。
 拜登先生还将在最大的跨国公司的全球收入增加新的最低税，并将通过内部收入服务对逃避税收的大型公司来加强执法努力。拜登先生周三表示，他拟议的税收对全球收入的变化将筹集1万亿美元超过15年。
 总统呼吁捍卫这些计划的税收公平感，他表示，该计划将对竞争中国竞争的挑战并展示民主资本主义仍然作品的世界提供具体的改善。
note_en:
WASHINGTON — President  Biden introduced a $2 trillion plan on Wednesday to overhaul and upgrade the nation’s infrastructure, calling it a transformational effort that could create the “most resilient, innovative economy in the world.”
 “It is not a plan that tinkers around the edges,” Mr. Biden said in a speech outside Pittsburgh. “It is a once-in-a-generation investment in America.”
 White House officials said the proposal’s combination of spending and tax credits would translate into 20,000 miles of rebuilt roads, repairs to the 10 most economically important bridges in the country, the elimination of lead pipes from the nation’s water supplies and a long list of other projects intended to create millions of jobs in the short run and strengthen American competitiveness in the long run.
 They said the plan would also accelerate the fight against climate change by hastening the shift to new, cleaner energy sources, and would help promote racial equality in the economy.
 The provisions would improve wages, internet service, drinking water and commute times, Mr. Biden said.
 The costs would be offset by increased corporate tax revenues raised over 15 years, particularly from multinationals that earn and book profits overseas. The president cast those increases as a means to prod companies into investing and producing more in the United States.
 With Republicans already signaling skepticism or outright opposition, Mr. Biden appealed for support from both parties in Congress, saying the program would be “unlike anything we have seen or done since we built the interstate highway system and the space race decades ago” and calling it “the largest American jobs investment since World War II.”
 The  spending in the plan would take place over eight years, the president said, and the tax increases would more than offset that spending in 15 years, leading to an eventual reduction of the budget deficit. Unlike the economic stimulus passed under President Barack Obama in 2009, when Mr. Biden was vice president, officials will not in every case prioritize so-called shovel-ready projects that could quickly bolster growth.
 But even spread over years, the scale of the proposal underscores how fully Mr. Biden has embraced the opportunity to use federal spending to address longstanding social and economic challenges in a way not seen in a half-century.
 Officials said that, if approved, the spending in the plan would  end decades of stagnation in federal investment in research and  infrastructure — and would return government investment in those areas, as a share of the economy, to its highest levels since the 1960s.
  The proposal is the first half of what will be a two-step release of the president’s ambitious agenda to overhaul the economy and remake American capitalism, which could carry a total cost of as much as $4 trillion over a decade. Mr. Biden’s administration has named it the “American Jobs Plan,” echoing the $1.9 trillion pandemic relief bill that Mr. Biden  signed into law this month, the “American Rescue Plan.”
 On Wednesday, Mr. Biden said the next phase, which he will seek to pay for in part through tax increases on wealthy individuals, would come in a matter of weeks and be known as the “American Family Plan.”
 While spending on roads, bridges and other physical improvements to the nation’s economic foundations has always had bipartisan appeal, the plan Mr. Biden rolled out on Wednesday drew quick opposition from the right for its size and its reliance on corporate tax increases.
 Republicans and business groups criticized those tax proposals, calling them nonstarters for bipartisan negotiations. Mr. Biden acknowledged the criticism, even as he defended asking companies to pay more in taxes. And he said he would continue to work on winning Republican support for his proposal.
 He said he had already spoken with Senator Mitch McConnell of Kentucky, the Republican leader, about the bill and planned to invite other Republicans to the White House as Congress turns to translating his proposal into detailed legislation.
 Mr. Biden challenged critics to offer their own proposals to pay for the plan. “I’m open to other ideas,” he said, “so long as they do not impose any tax increase on people making less than $400,000.”
 Mr. McConnell suggested on Wednesday he was unlikely to support the package in the form being proposed by the White House.
 “If it’s going to have massive tax increases and trillions more added to the national debt, it’s not likely,” he said.
  The spending in the plan covers a wide range of physical infrastructure projects, including transportation, broadband, the electric grid and housing; efforts to jump-start advanced manufacturing; and other industries officials see as key to the United States’ growing economic competition with China.
 It also includes money to train millions of workers, as well as money for initiatives to support labor unions and providers of in-home care for older and disabled Americans, while also increasing the pay of the workers who provide that care.
 Many of the items in the plan carry price tags that would have filled entire, ambitious bills in past administrations.
 Among them: a total of $180 billion for research and development, $115 billion for roads and bridges, $85 billion for public transit, and $80 billion for Amtrak and freight rail. There is $42 billion for ports and airports, $100 billion for broadband and $111 billion for water infrastructure — including $45 billion to ensure no child ever is forced to drink water from a lead pipe, which can slow children’s development and lead to behavioral and other problems.
  The plan seeks to repair 10,000 smaller bridges across the country, along with the 10 most economically significant ones in need of a fix. It would electrify 20 percent of the nation’s fleet of yellow school buses. And it would spend $300 billion to promote advanced manufacturing, including a four-year plan to restock the country’s Strategic National Stockpile of pharmaceuticals, including vaccines, in preparation for future pandemics.
 In many cases, officials cast those goals in the language of closing racial gaps in the economy, sometimes the result of previous federal spending efforts, like interstate highway developments that split communities of color or air pollution that affects Black and Hispanic communities near ports or power plants.
 Officials cast the $400 billion spending on in-home care in part as a salve to “underpaid and undervalued” workers in that industry, who are disproportionately women of color.
  Mr. Biden’s pledge to tackle climate change is embedded throughout the plan. Roads, bridges and airports would be made more resilient to the effects of more extreme storms, floods and fires wrought by a warming planet. Spending on research and development could help spur breakthroughs in cutting-edge clean technology, while plans to retrofit and weatherize millions of buildings would make them more energy efficient.
 The president’s focus on climate change is centered, however, on modernizing and transforming the United States’ two largest sources of planet-warming greenhouse gas pollution: cars and electric power plants.
 It bets heavily on spending meant to increase the use of electric cars, which today make up just 2 percent of the vehicles on America’s highways.
 The plan proposes spending $174 billion to encourage the manufacture and purchase of electric vehicles by granting tax credits and other incentives to companies that make electric vehicle batteries in the United States instead of China. The goal is to reduce vehicle price tags.
  The money would also fund the construction of about a half-million electric vehicle charging stations — although experts say that number is but a tiny fraction of what is needed to make electric vehicles a mainstream option.
 Mr. Biden’s plan proposes $100 billion in programs to update and modernize the electric grid to make it more reliable and less susceptible to blackouts, like those that recently devastated Texas, while also building more transmission lines from wind and solar plants to large cities.
 It proposes the creation of a “Clean Electricity Standard” — essentially, a federal mandate requiring that a certain percentage of electricity in the United States be generated by zero-carbon energy sources like wind, solar and possibly nuclear power. But that mandate would have to be enacted by Congress, where prospects for its success remain murky. Similar efforts to pass such a mandate have failed multiple times over the past 20 years.
 The plan proposes an additional $46 billion in federal procurement programs for government agencies to buy fleets of electric vehicles, and $35 billion in research and development programs for cutting-edge, new technologies.
 It also calls for making infrastructure and communities more prepared for the worsening effects of climate change, though the administration has so far provided few details on how it would accomplish that goal.
 But according to the document released by the White House, the plan includes $50 billion “in dedicated investments to improve infrastructure resilience.” The efforts would defend against wildfires, rising seas and hurricanes, and there would be a focus on investments that protect low-income residents and people of color.
 The plan also includes a $16 billion program intended to help fossil fuel workers transition to new work — like capping leaks on defunct oil wells and shutting down retired coal mines — and $10 billion for a new “Civilian Climate Corps.”
  Mr. Biden would fund his spending in part by eliminating tax preferences for fossil fuel producers. But the bulk of his tax increases would come from corporations generally.
 He would raise the corporate tax rate to 28 percent from 21 percent, partly reversing a cut signed into law by President Donald J. Trump. Mr. Biden would also take a variety of steps to raise taxes on multinational corporations, many of them working within an overhaul of the taxation of profits earned overseas that was included in Mr. Trump’s tax law in 2017.
 Those measures would include raising the rate of a minimum tax on global profits and eliminating several provisions that allow companies to reduce their American tax liability on profits they earn and book abroad.
 Mr. Biden would also add a new minimum tax on the global income of the largest multinationals, and he would ramp up enforcement efforts by the Internal Revenue Service against large companies that evade taxes. Mr. Biden said on Wednesday that his proposed tax changes on global income alone would raise $1 trillion over 15 years.
 The president appealed to a sense of tax fairness in defending those plans, and he said the plan would deliver concrete improvements to standards of living for Americans while meeting the challenge of competition from China and showing the world that democratic capitalism still works.
title: Biden详细信息2万亿美元的计划重建基础设施并重塑经济
title_en: Biden Details $2 Trillion Plan To Rebuild Infrastructure and Reshape the Economy

  亚马逊发言人告诉BBC，达拉不是官方大使，但没有回应BBC对其他几个账户澄清的要求。

note_en:
‘Fake' accounts claiming to be Amazon workers have been praising their working conditions on Twitter.
 Votes are currently being counted in Alabama to decide whether Amazon warehouse workers will form a union.
 But last night, a series of anti-union tweets were sent from accounts claiming to be staff.
 Twitter has now suspended many of the accounts, and Amazon has confirmed at least one is fake.
 Most of the accounts were made just a few days ago, often with only a few tweets, all related to Amazon.
 “What bothers me most about unions is there’s no ability to opt out of dues,” one user under the handle @AmazonFCDarla tweeted, despite a state law in Alabama which prevents this.
    Another account - which later changed its profile picture after it was revealed to be fake - said: “Unions are good for some companies, but I don’t want to have to shell out hundreds a month just for lawyers!”
    Amazon has previously used this handle for its so-called Amazon Ambassadors - real employees who are paid by the firm to promote and defend it on Twitter.
  An Amazon spokesperson told the BBC that Darla was not an official Ambassador, but has not responded to the BBC’s request for clarification on several other accounts.
 “It appears that this is a fake account that violates Twitter’s terms,” the spokesperson said. “We’ve asked Twitter to investigate and take appropriate action.”
   Several of the high-profile accounts have been suspended by Twitter. It told the BBC that Amazon Ambassadors are subject to Twitter's rules on spam and platform manipulation.
 Accounts which impersonate or falsely claim to be affiliated with a company, can be temporarily suspended or removed.
  It is unclear whether the accounts are real employees, bots or trolls pretending to be Amazon Ambassadors.
   Amazon’s executives and its official accounts have been tweeting in defence of the company in recent days, after negative reports of poor working conditions, including staff having to urinate in bottles.
  The official Amazon News account tweeted: “You don’t really believe the peeing in bottles thing, do you? If that were true, nobody would work for us.
  “The truth is that we have over a million incredible employees around the world who are proud of what they do, and have great wages and health care from day one.”
  In response to a tweet by US Democrat senator Elizabeth Warren, which accused Amazon of “exploiting loopholes and tax havens”, the account said:
 “You make the tax laws, we just follow them. If you don’t like the laws you’ve created, by all means change them.
 “Here are the facts: Amazon has paid billions of dollars in corporate taxes over the past few years alone.”
title: '假'亚马逊工人在推特上捍卫公司
title_en: 'Fake' Amazon Workers Defend Company on Twitter
